paper.title_en: Astrophysical signatures of black holes in beta dark matter halos: QPO constraints from X-ray binaries, shadow, accretion, and thermal radiation
paper.abstract_en: We examine the geodesic structure, shadow, quasi-periodic oscillations (QPOs), thermodynamic properties, and greybody factors (GBFs) of a static, spherically symmetric black hole (BH) embedded in a beta dark matter halo (DMH). The photon sphere, shadow radius, and photon trajectories grow with increasing DMH density, producing deviations that can be tested against Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) bounds for Sgr A* and M87*. For massive particles, we calculate the innermost stable circular orbit (ISCO), the radiative efficiency, and the epicyclic frequencies, and we read off the epicyclic resonance (ER) model predictions for twin-peak QPOs. With the emcee Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) sampler, these predictions are compared with observed QPO data from three stellar-mass X-ray binaries (XTE J1550–564, GRO J1655–40, GRS 1915+105), the supermassive BH Sgr A*, and the intermediate-mass candidate M82 X-1, yielding 1σ constraints on the BH mass and the DMH parameter κ for all five sources. The thermodynamic analysis gives a single phase transition whose critical horizon shifts as the halo parameters vary, while the spin-0 GBFs show that the DMH lowers the potential barrier and lets more low-frequency scalar emission through. The beta DMH thus leaves measurable signatures in X-ray timing, shadow imaging, accretion efficiency, and BH ringdown spectra.
